proposed_hypotheses.text: Some baseline features are strongly associated with pfs_months on a univariate basis; specifically, the continuous variable feature_078 (range 30-90, plausibly age) is positively associated with pfs_months.
The 3-level variable feature_057 is ordinally and inversely associated with pfs_months (higher level -> lower pfs_months), consistent with a performance-status- or stage-like variable.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; stats.spearmanr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho between feature_078 and pfs_months = +0.785 (p<1e-300). Decile means rise monotonically from 1.33 months (age 30-52) to 7.45 months (age 78-90).

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); stats.kruskal(...)
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: feature_057=0 -> 5.27 (n=17592), =1 -> 4.05 (n=24971), =2 -> 2.91 (n=7437). Kruskal-Wallis p<1e-300; clear ordinal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_051 (prevalence 55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months (mean PFS lower when feature_051=1 than when feature_051=0).
Binary feature_038 (prevalence 20%) is associated with longer pfs_months (mean PFS higher when feature_038=1 than when feature_038=0); plausibly a treatment.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_051==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_051==1,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 5.05 (feature_051=0) vs 3.70 (feature_051=1); difference -1.35 months, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.feature_038==0,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df.feature_038==1,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS = 4.12 (feature_038=0) vs 5.09 (feature_038=1); difference +0.97 months, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_013 (prevalence 42%) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for age (feature_078), feature_051, and feature_057.
Binary feature_043 (prevalence 35%) is independently associated with shorter pfs_months after adjustment for age (feature_078), feature_051, and feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038 + feature_013 + feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS: feature_013 coef -0.353 (p<1e-300); feature_043 coef -0.287 (p<1e-300). Both negative and significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_038 (the candidate treatment) on pfs_months is heterogeneous: feature_038 confers more benefit in patients with feature_013=0 than in those with feature_013=1 (i.e., feature_013 is a marker of resistance to the feature_038 treatment).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_013', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_038 main effect (in feature_013=0): +1.63 months (p<1e-300). feature_038 x feature_013 interaction: -1.63 months (p<1e-300). In feature_013=1 patients, feature_038's net effect is essentially zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 is also a marker of resistance to feature_038: the benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is smaller (or absent) in patients with feature_043=1 than in those with feature_043=0.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 main effect (in feature_043=0): +1.45 months (p<1e-300). feature_038 x feature_043 interaction: -1.46 months (p<1e-300). feature_043 abolishes the benefit of feature_038.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_109, and feature_067 are mutually exclusive (patients have at most one of these markers), consistent with three different mutually-exclusive states of a single underlying biomarker (e.g., three different mutations of the same gene).

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['feature_109']); pd.crosstab(df['feature_013'], df['feature_067']); pd.crosstab(df['feature_109'], df['feature_067'])
analyses.result_summary: All pairwise (1,1) cells are zero: feature_013 & feature_109: 0/50000; feature_013 & feature_067: 0/50000; feature_109 & feature_067: 0/50000. Mutual exclusivity confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 is another resistance marker for feature_038: the benefit of feature_038 on pfs_months is reduced (or even reversed) in feature_109=1 patients vs feature_109=0 patients.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038*feature_109', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 x feature_109 interaction = -0.97 months (p=6.1e-80). Among feature_109=1 patients, feature_038 confers no benefit; raw means show 4.14 (untreated) -> 3.94 (treated).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Unlike feature_013/043/109, feature_067 is associated with INCREASED benefit from feature_038: feature_038 x feature_067 interaction is positive (a sensitivity rather than resistance marker).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_038*feature_067', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 x feature_067 interaction = +0.91 months (p=3.5e-48). In feature_067=1 patients, feature_038 raises mean PFS from 4.13 to 6.05 (vs 4.12 -> 5.06 in feature_067=0 patients).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining the 'eligible' subgroup as feature_013=0 AND feature_043=0 AND feature_109=0 (a 'wild-type-like' subgroup, prevalence ~35%), feature_038 confers a substantial pfs_months benefit in this subgroup but not in the complementary 'mutant-like' subgroup.

analyses.code: df['ras_wt']=((df.feature_013==0)&(df.feature_043==0)&(df.feature_109==0)).astype(int); smf.ols(... feature_038, data=sub_wt) ; smf.ols(... feature_038, data=sub_mt)
analyses.result_summary: Within wild-type-like subgroup (n=17372): adjusted feature_038 effect = +2.74 months (p<1e-300). Within mutant-like subgroup (n=32628): adjusted feature_038 effect = -0.003 (p=0.35, NS). The treatment is restricted-benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, feature_043, and feature_109 are not strongly prognostic on their own: among feature_038=0 (untreated) patients, mean pfs_months is similar across mutation subgroups (i.e., these markers are predictive of treatment response, not prognostic of disease course).

analyses.code: df[df.feature_038==0].groupby(['feature_013','feature_043','feature_109'])['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months among feature_038=0 patients: WT 4.12; feature_013-only 4.12; feature_043-only 4.12; feature_109-only 4.14; double-positive 4.10. Range = 0.04 months. The markers are not prognostic in untreated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_092 (range 1.5-5.5; plausibly serum albumin) is positively and independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment for other major prognostic factors.
The continuous variable feature_099 (range 0-24.6) is negatively and independently associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_092 adjusted coef = +0.471 per unit (p<1e-300; ~+0.24 months per SD). feature_099 adjusted coef = -0.077 per unit (p<1e-300; ~-0.30 months per SD).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_009 (continuous, broad range 0.02-777, plausibly CEA) is independently and negatively associated with pfs_months after adjusting for the other major prognostic factors.
feature_006 (continuous, range 48-830) is independently and negatively associated with pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043 + feature_038*feature_109 + feature_009 + feature_006', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_009 adjusted coef = -0.0048 per unit (p=3.9e-124; per-SD effect approx -0.08 months). feature_006 coef = -0.0005 per unit (p=2.3e-33; per-SD effect approx -0.04 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for feature_038 and the resistance/sensitivity markers (feature_013/043/109/067), few other binary features have a meaningful (>0.1 month) main effect on pfs_months — feature_038 is the dominant therapeutic intervention.

analyses.code: for each binary feature: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covariates and feature_038 interactions> + c', data=df).fit().params[c]
analyses.result_summary: Across 80 binary features, all adjusted coefficients are |b| < 0.03 months apart from feature_051, feature_038, and the four mutation markers. No additional latent treatment with effect >|0.05 months| was detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no race-based disparity in pfs_months: after adjustment, mean pfs_months does not differ across feature_064 categories (white/black/hispanic/asian/other).
There is no insurance-based disparity in pfs_months: after adjustment, mean pfs_months does not differ across feature_018 categories.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covariates> + C(feature_064) + C(feature_018)', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All race coefficients |b|<0.02 months, all p>0.50. All insurance coefficients |b|<0.03 months, all p>0.21. No detectable disparity in pfs_months by race or insurance after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like (feature_013=0, feature_043=0, feature_109=0) subgroup, the rate of feature_038 use does not differ by race (feature_064) — i.e., treatment access is equitable across race in eligible patients.
Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the rate of feature_038 use does not differ by insurance (feature_018).

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(sub_wt['feature_064'], sub_wt['feature_038']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 use rate by race in WT-like subgroup: white 19.9%, black 20.4%, hispanic 19.3%, asian 21.4%, other 18.5%. Chi-square = 2.73, p = 0.60. No disparity.

analyses.code: stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(sub_wt['feature_018'], sub_wt['feature_038']))
analyses.result_summary: feature_038 use rate by insurance: medicare 19.7%, private 20.2%, medicaid 19.9%, uninsured 19.4%. Chi-square = 0.90, p = 0.83. No disparity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038 does NOT differ by race (no race x feature_038 interaction).

Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038 is reduced in uninsured patients compared to medicaid baseline (a small but detectable disparity in treatment benefit).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covariates> + C(feature_064)*feature_038', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Race x feature_038 interaction terms: black +0.030 (p=0.78), hispanic +0.093 (p=0.36), asian (ref), other +0.302 (p=0.08), white -0.056 (p=0.53). No statistically significant interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ <covariates> + C(feature_018)*feature_038', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Insurance x feature_038 interactions vs medicaid: medicare -0.061 (p=0.35), private -0.094 (p=0.16), uninsured -0.377 (p=0.0021). Uninsured patients derive ~0.4 fewer months of benefit; small subgroup (n=687) but statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038 is similar across feature_057 (ECOG-like) levels: even patients with the highest feature_057 level derive substantial benefit.

analyses.code: sub_wt.groupby(['feature_057','feature_038'])['pfs_months'].mean(); smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_038*C(feature_057) + ...', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Treated-vs-untreated PFS difference within WT-like, by feature_057: 0 -> +2.67 months; 1 -> +2.77; 2 -> +2.74. Interaction terms feature_038 x feature_057: 1 vs 0 = -0.087 (p=0.06), 2 vs 0 = -0.069 (p=0.30). Benefit is essentially constant across performance status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038 increases slightly with age (feature_078): a small positive feature_038 x feature_078 interaction.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_078', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_078 = +0.0062 per year (p=0.0038). Direction is positive (older = larger benefit), but very small in magnitude (about +0.06 months extra benefit per decade).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the wild-type-like subgroup, the absolute pfs_months benefit of feature_038 decreases as feature_092 (albumin-like) rises -- patients with already-high feature_092 derive less incremental benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_092', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_092 = -0.124 per unit (p=0.0043). Negative interaction, but absolute magnitude is small relative to the overall +2.7-month main effect of feature_038 in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 and feature_043 are partially overlapping in their effect-modifying role: in patients who have BOTH feature_013=1 and feature_043=1, the joint negative effect on pfs_months is sub-additive (interaction term feature_013:feature_043 is positive).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_013*feature_043', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_013:feature_043 interaction = +0.50 (p=1.0e-148). Sub-additivity confirmed: when both markers are present, additional negative effect is ~0.5 months less than expected from independent main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The multi-level features (feature_025, feature_075, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033) are weakly associated or not associated with pfs_months -- they are not major prognostic or treatment variables.

analyses.code: df.groupby(<each multi feature>)['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Range of pfs_months across categories: feature_025 4.23-4.36; feature_075 4.29-4.34; feature_071 4.19-4.40; feature_026 4.28-4.32; feature_096 4.26-4.35; feature_033 4.19-4.34. All differences <0.2 months; not substantively associated with PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 has a slight effect modification on feature_038 benefit: patients with feature_051=1 derive a marginally larger absolute pfs_months gain from feature_038 than feature_051=0 patients (positive feature_038:feature_051 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_038*feature_051', data=sub_wt).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_038:feature_051 interaction = +0.166 (p=1.0e-4). Modest positive interaction; feature_051=1 (worse-prognosis) patients gain slightly more from feature_038 than feature_051=0 patients within the WT-like subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the larger continuous variables, the relative ranking of independent prognostic strength (per SD on pfs_months, after full adjustment) is feature_078 (age) >> feature_092 > feature_099 > feature_009 > feature_006.

analyses.code: compute coef * SD for each continuous covariate in fully-adjusted OLS
analyses.result_summary: Per-SD effect on pfs_months (months): feature_078 +1.75; feature_092 +0.24; feature_099 -0.30; feature_009 -0.08; feature_006 -0.04. feature_078 dominates; the others contribute small but real prognostic information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the wild-type-like subgroup, addition of feature_067=1 on top of feature_038 treatment provides only a marginal incremental pfs_months gain compared to feature_038 alone (since feature_067 itself is already part of the wild-type-like sensitive group).

analyses.code: sub_wt.groupby(['feature_067','feature_038'])['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Within WT-like: feature_038=0/feature_067=0 -> 4.117; feature_038=1/feature_067=0 -> 6.866; feature_038=0/feature_067=1 -> 4.112; feature_038=1/feature_067=1 -> 7.018. feature_067=1 adds ~+0.15 months on top of feature_038 within this already-sensitive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An overall summary model -- pfs_months regressed on feature_078 + feature_051 + C(feature_057) + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_009 + feature_006 + feature_038*feature_013 + feature_038*feature_043 + feature_038*feature_109 + feature_038*feature_067 -- captures the dominant prognostic and predictive structure of the dataset; R^2 should approach the ceiling implied by feature_078 alone (which had univariate rho ~0.78).

analyses.code: smf.ols(<full summary formula>, data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Full summary OLS R^2 approx 0.86. The model is dominated by feature_078 (age-like) and the feature_038 x mutation interactions; remaining variance is largely irreducible noise around the conditional mean. The structure is consistent with a single targeted treatment whose response is gated by mutually exclusive resistance markers (feature_013/043/109) and modestly enhanced by a sensitivity marker (feature_067), against a backdrop of standard prognostic variables (age, performance status, disease severity, albumin-like, inflammation-like, CEA-like).
